steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank completes a sentence in which a photography festival location is named and then a second independent clause follows: "the exhibited photographs... captivated audiences." Two independent clauses must be joined correctly, and "for example" is a parenthetical phrase modifying the location.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'Italy, for example;': The comma before "for example" and the semicolon after it correctly set off the parenthetical and then join the two independent clauses. ✓
  • Choice B — 'Italy, for example,': Uses only a comma after "for example" to separate two independent clauses — a comma splice. ✗
  • Choice C — 'Italy; for example,': The semicolon after "Italy" severs the location from "for example," turning it into a transitional phrase rather than a parenthetical clarifying the location. ✗
  • Choice D — 'Italy, for example': No punctuation at all after "for example" — the two independent clauses run together. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
